rationale: Both abstracts define a specific causal gap and fit CircAE well through arrhythmia susceptibility, ECG, patch clamp, optical mapping, and ion channel phenotyping. Both are weak on results rigor because neither reports sample sizes, effect sizes, exact comparators for every endpoint, confidence intervals, or p values. Y is stronger overall because it is more concise, names wild type littermates as comparators, avoids some of X's more expansive causal and therapeutic language, and reads closer to a journal ready structured abstract.
loser_weakness: X most lacked numerical result reporting and calibration. It makes broad claims about etiological causality, regulatory homeostasis, and therapeutic window without quantitative support in the abstract.
